findings.title: Foundry broadcast run history is committed as source
findings.why_it_matters: The branch adds five generated broadcast files for the same deployment script, including timestamped runs plus run-latest. These files contain full transaction bytecode, calldata, nonces, gas, and hashes, so every rebroadcast or redeploy creates thousands of noisy lines and merge conflicts while obscuring the small set of deployment facts maintainers need.
evidence: The diff adds run-1778692290802.json, run-1778801345964.json, run-1778802068226.json, run-1778811808539.json, and run-latest.json under broadcast/DeployFameRouter.s.sol/8453.
run-latest.json starts with generated transaction payloads at line 1 and includes full CREATE bytecode input in the first transaction.
findings.suggested_fix: Keep a curated deployment record in docs/config or commit one canonical, intentionally reviewed artifact. Exclude transient broadcast run history and run-latest from the product branch unless the repository has an explicit policy that these generated logs are release artifacts.

findings.title: Stale CE review and todo artifacts are part of the product diff
findings.why_it_matters: The branch adds prior review outputs and todos under .context as checked-in project content. At least one of those artifacts now describes obsolete code, which leaves future maintainers sorting stale agent state from source changes and can make reviews re-litigate issues that have already been fixed.
evidence: The added 20260511 maintainability artifact claims venue adapters all call a mock-style executeLeg path, but the current changed adapters call concrete router methods such as swapExactTokensForTokens and exactInputSingle.
The diff also adds ten .context/compound-engineering/todos markdown files with workflow state unrelated to the router runtime or compiler sources.
findings.suggested_fix: Keep CE runtime artifacts out of the branch, or move only intentionally durable decisions into normal docs. Do not delete protected docs/brainstorms, docs/plans, or docs/solutions artifacts.

findings.title: Route catalog data is embedded inside compiler control flow
findings.why_it_matters: candidateRoutes is a large hardcoded catalog of route IDs, funding policy, pinned quote amounts, token paths, and capability labels inside the compiler module. That couples route curation to compiler logic: adding or retiring a supported route now requires editing code, generated fixtures, and tests together, even when the compiler behavior has not changed.
evidence: candidateRoutes starts at line 187 and immediately asserts specific pool IDs, pinned quote constants, and a long array of production/proof routes.
compileRoutes only maps those embedded candidates through buildCompiledRoute, so the module is both catalog and compiler.
findings.suggested_fix: Split the route catalog from the compiler. Keep compileRoute focused on validating and encoding a typed candidate route, and load the supported route set from a fixture/data module that can be regenerated or reviewed independently.

findings.title: Deploy configuration ignores the generated solver manifest
findings.why_it_matters: The branch introduces a generated FameRouterSolverFixtureManifest with required venue targets and V4 hook-data keys, but the deployment script configures the router only from the hand-maintained FameRouterFixtureManifest. This creates two sources of launch configuration truth and makes future generated solver routes easy to validate in tests but omit from deployed router setup.
evidence: DeployFameRouter imports FameRouterFixtureManifest at line 7 and _configureVenueTargets iterates only FameRouterFixtureManifest.requiredVenueTargetCount at line 41.

FameRouterSolverFixtureManifest exposes requiredVenueTargetCount and requiredV4HookDataKeyCount, but rg shows those hook-data accessors are only used by tests, not by deployment or validation scripts.
findings.suggested_fix: Generate or expose one launch manifest consumed by deploy, validation, and fork tests, or explicitly compose the base fixture manifest with the generated solver manifest in deployment, including setV4HookDataHashEnabled for generated hook-data keys.

residual_risks: This maintainability pass was read-only aside from writing the requested artifact; no tests or generators were run.
The diff is large, so this review focused on changed router/compiler/deploy/generated-artifact surfaces rather than exhaustively auditing every test helper branch.
testing_gaps: No verification was run to prove broadcast and .context artifact cleanup would leave release validation intact.
No generator refactor was attempted, so route catalog separation would need follow-up parity checks across router:generate:check and Forge fixture tests.
